name: atlas-pipeline-ssot
description: ATLAS pipeline SSOT direct-write template: write YAML first, repair, validate, handoff

tasks.detail: Use the orchestrator goal and ATLAS workflow context to write a compact starter <ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml. Do not inspect repository files before the first write unless the path is impossible. Include top_module, sub_modules, parameters, io_list, features, dataflow, function_model, cycle_model, registers, interrupts, fsm, custom.assumptions, test_requirements, quality_gates, and workflow_todos. Keep the first write small enough to complete quickly; repair_ssot_schema.py will expand canonical sections after disk evidence exists.
tasks.criteria:
<ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml is written by a real file-write tool
The first YAML is a compact starter, not a full generated book
Non-reset function_model transactions include output_rules or state_updates
Submodules include ownership refs
Assumptions are recorded under custom.assumptions
No RTL/TB/sim/lint/coverage/EDA artifacts are written by ssot-gen

tasks.detail: Run python3 workflow/ssot-gen/scripts/repair_ssot_schema.py <ip> --mode engineering, then bash workflow/ssot-gen/scripts/check_ssot_disk.sh <ip> --mode engineering. Inspect failures literally and repair the YAML if needed.
tasks.criteria:
repair_ssot_schema.py executed
check_ssot_disk.sh executed with --mode engineering
YAML parses successfully
Validation output is captured
Any remaining failure is reported as a blocker, not green

tasks.detail: Emit a compact [SSOT HANDOFF] for rtl-gen. Include the exact SSOT path, top module, clock/reset, interfaces, registers/CSR policy, function/cycle model summary, assumptions, validation evidence, unresolved gaps, and downstream owners.
tasks.criteria:
[SSOT HANDOFF] block is emitted
Handoff names rtl-gen as next owner
Handoff does not claim RTL, TB, sim, lint, waveform, coverage, synthesis, STA, PnR, or signoff completion
